scope_note: v0.4.0 declares this baseline as per-language; v0.5.0 will move to baselines/go/coverage-baselines.json (shape B). Example_* count is a Go-specific concept — Python equivalent is doctest, Rust is #[example]; per-language adapter materializes the metric.
note: Per-package branch coverage + Example_* count baselines. New packages default to 90% branch floor (CLAUDE.md Rule #14). Example count tracked per package as a compensating baseline for retired golden-corpus (Rule 28) — a drop vs baseline with ≥2 prior runs emits a WARN in learning-notifications.md. Raise only if improvement >5%; regressions keep baseline and flag. Reset every 5 runs.

core/l2cache/dragonfly.example_count_baseline_note: First baseline will be captured on the next run that touches this package — metrics-collector step 8.5 emits the count.

raise_policy.description: Raise baseline on >5% improvement. Flag >5% regression. Never lower without learning-engine authorization.

example_count_policy.description: Example_* count per package. Raise-only on increase. Drop vs baseline with ≥2 prior runs emits a WARN line in runs/<run-id>/feedback/learning-notifications.md (no gate; H10 reviewer decides). Compensating baseline for retired golden-corpus.
